_meta.schema: Each plan: {id, title, subtitle, category, days, icon, color, source, schedule: [{day, theme, reflection, verseRefs:[]}]}. category drives the chip filter on the Plans screen. Selecting a plan calls personalizationService.saveActivePlan with these fields converted to ReadingPlan + PlanDay.

love_talk.title: Love Talk
love_talk.subtitle: Daily devotionals for couples to grow together in love and faith

schedule.reflection: Read 1 Corinthians 13 aloud together. Share one moment from this past month when your spouse was patient with you. Thank them.
God designed marriage to make two distinct people one in covenant. Talk about one area where you've grown closer this year — and one where you'd like to grow.
Submission isn't weakness; it's strength laid down for love. Each spouse: name one way you can honor the other this week, then pray for grace to follow through.
Words can wound or heal. Confess one harsh word from this week. Speak one word of praise over your spouse before you sleep.
Forgive as the Lord forgave you. If there's an unspoken hurt, name it gently and forgive it tonight — don't let the sun go down on your anger.
Proverbs 31 isn't a checklist — it's a portrait of trust. Read it together and tell your spouse one strength you see in them that the world overlooks.
The Song of Songs celebrates the joy of married love. Spend tonight delighting — laugh, hold hands, pray together, dream about the year ahead.

married_for_god.title: Married for God
married_for_god.subtitle: A 14-day journey to anchor your marriage in Scripture's blueprint

schedule.reflection: Read Genesis 1-2 together. Marriage is God's idea, not culture's. Take time to thank God for designing this covenant.

schedule.theme: A picture of Christ and the Church
schedule.reflection: Marriage models the gospel. Husbands love sacrificially; wives respond with respect. Both reflect Christ.
Covenant means 'no exit clauses.' Discuss what 'till death do us part' really means in seasons of difficulty.
James 1:19 is marriage gold. Practice today: each spouse shares one thing weighing on them; the other listens without solving.
Honesty wrapped in tenderness builds trust. Avoid both flattery and harshness this week.

schedule.theme: Don't let the sun go down
schedule.reflection: Resolve conflict same-day when possible. Apologize specifically. Forgive completely.
Patience in marriage means allowing room for your spouse to grow. List 3 ways your spouse has grown since you met.
Couples who pray together build something the enemy can't easily break. Begin a 5-minute nightly prayer rhythm.
Your home is a sanctuary. Sing one worship song together tonight, even if only one of you carries the tune.
Serve each other before you serve anyone outside the home. Do one unrequested act of service for your spouse today.
Protect your marriage from outside affection, secret comparisons, and digital distraction. Set one practical guardrail tonight.
Whether or not you have children, your marriage testifies to those watching. Discuss what story your marriage is telling.
Every marriage faces seasons of pain. God's grace is sufficient — endurance produces character; character, hope.
Marriage on earth is a shadow of the marriage of the Lamb. Renew your vows tonight in your own words. Look forward to eternity together with Christ.

praying_for_spouse.title: Praying for Your Spouse
praying_for_spouse.subtitle: 21 days of Spirit-led prayer over the one you love

schedule.reflection: Pray your spouse hungers and thirsts after righteousness today.
Pray for wisdom in every word, choice, and silence today.
Pray they would find joy in trials and not be overwhelmed.
Pray temptation would have no foothold; pray for accountability and grace.
Pray anxiety would be replaced with the peace of God.
Pray their speech would build others up; that no idle word be spoken in haste.
Pray they would do everything as unto the Lord; that their labor would not be in vain.
Pray for physical strength, mental clarity, and Sabbath rest in their week.
Pray they would be cheerful givers, never bound by greed or fear.
Pray patience with you, with children, with strangers, with God's timing.
Pray they would not be ashamed of the gospel; that the Spirit would give them courage.
Pray humility to receive correction; quickness to admit when wrong.
Pray they would draw deep joy from God's presence today, not from circumstances.
Pray they would test every spirit; recognize truth from counterfeit.
Pray their heart would break for what breaks God's heart; pray for kindness to flow easily.
Pray over food, screens, anger, ambition — that the fruit of the Spirit would govern, not the flesh.
Pray they would be faithful in the small, hidden things; that their integrity would be unshakeable.
Pray a grateful heart in all circumstances; pray complaint would not take root.
Pray for a Jonathan to your David — for godly friends who sharpen them.
Pray over the desires of their heart — that the Lord would either fulfill or transform them.
Pray they would set their mind on things above; that today's troubles would feel light against eternal weight of glory.

raising_kids.title: Raising Kids in the Word
raising_kids.subtitle: Two weeks for parents anchoring their family in Scripture

schedule.reflection: View your children today as God's reward, not a burden. Speak that aloud over them.
Talk about Scripture in ordinary moments — when you sit, walk, lie down, rise.
Each child has a unique design from God. Pray for discernment to train them in their way.
Loving discipline is not anger; it shapes character. Apologize where you've crossed into anger.
Bless your children with words today — name what you see God doing in them.
Father, don't crush your children's spirit. Listen first; correct gently; never mock.
Pray AT bedtime WITH each child by name. Let them hear you intercede for them.
Your kids learn the gospel by watching you live it. Repent quickly when you fail.
Every prayer you pray today shapes children, grandchildren, great-grandchildren.
Carve out one full evening as family Sabbath. Worship, eat slowly, ditch phones.
Welcome a friend, neighbor, or stranger to your table this week. Let your kids serve.
Teach kids to honor authority — yours, teachers', the Lord's. Model honoring your own parents.
Each child must own their faith. Stop forcing; start inviting. Pray for the Spirit to draw them.
Recommit your home today: 'As for me and my house, we will serve the LORD.' Write it on something visible.

anxiety_to_peace.title: Anxiety into Peace
anxiety_to_peace.subtitle: Two weeks of Scripture to quiet a worried heart

schedule.reflection: He cares about the thing that woke you up at 3am. Name it specifically and hand it over.
Birds and lilies. If God dresses the field, He will dress your tomorrow too.
Stop planning. Stop scrolling. Sit in silence for 10 minutes today and let God be God.
Read Psalm 23 aloud, slowly, three times. Let each phrase pour over you.
Fear feeds on the lie that you're alone. You are perfectly loved by the Father.
Walk by faith today, not by what circumstances suggest. Faith reads ahead of evidence.
Tomorrow's grace isn't yours yet. You only need today's. He will meet you again at sunrise.
Lead me to the rock that is higher than I. Pray that prayer for someone else today, too.
List three fears. For each, write 'God is greater than this' and one verse beside it.
Tonight, give the day to Him before your head hits the pillow. He gives His beloved sleep.
Anxiety lies. Hold each anxious thought against this list — true, noble, lovely, praiseworthy.
His peace is not the world's peace. It doesn't depend on circumstances changing.
Out of the depths, you cry. He inclines His ear. He has not forgotten you.
Look back at how He brought you through. Build a small altar of remembrance for tomorrow's storms.

first_steps.title: First Steps with Jesus
first_steps.subtitle: 21 days of essentials for a new follower of Christ

schedule.reflection: Read John 3 slowly. Underline every phrase about God's love.

schedule.theme: I am the bread of life
schedule.reflection: Jesus is the food your soul has been hungry for. Daily.

schedule.theme: I am the light of the world
schedule.reflection: He shines into every dark corner. Invite Him into one you've kept hidden.
He knows your name. He laid down His life for you specifically.
Death has lost its sting. Sit with that truth.
Not a way; the Way. Not a truth; the Truth. Not a life; THE Life.
Stay connected to Him today — even one minute of prayer rooted you deeper.
Not by your works — by His. Receive that today.
Old things are passed away. The voice of shame is no longer your master.
You are righteous in Christ — declared not guilty.
You are not alone today. The same Spirit that raised Christ is in you.
Read Romans 8:31-39 aloud. Memorize verse 37.
Jesus taught simply. Try praying the Lord's Prayer slowly today.
Bring an actual request to Him today. Speak it out loud.
All the law and prophets — every command — hangs on these two.
Is there someone you've held bitterness against? Today is the day to release them.
Move at least one priority today from earth's ledger to heaven's.
Following Jesus costs everything — and gains everything.
Pray today for one person you can introduce to Jesus this month.
You weren't meant to follow Jesus alone. Find or commit to a local church.
Live today like Jesus might step through the clouds tomorrow. Watch and pray.

comfort_in_grief.title: Comfort in Grief
comfort_in_grief.subtitle: Fourteen days of Scripture for the brokenhearted

schedule.reflection: He is close to the brokenhearted. He doesn't watch from afar.
Jesus stood at Lazarus's tomb and wept. Your tears today are not weakness; they are holy.
Don't rush yourself. Grief has a season. Honor it.
He comforts us so that we can later comfort others. Your sorrow is not wasted.
Even in the valley of the shadow, you fear no evil — for He is with you.
All. Not just the small ones. Hand Him the whole weight tonight.
Joy comes in the morning — even if morning takes years. He is faithful.
When you can't form words, He hears the groaning of your heart. Just sit with Him today.
His grace is sufficient. His power is made perfect in your weakness, not despite it.
What you suffer now is preparing in you a weight of glory beyond all comparison.

schedule.theme: We do not grieve as others
schedule.reflection: Christian grief is honest and deep — but it is not without hope.
He gently leads those that are with young. He carries you when your legs give out.
He gives oil of joy for mourning, garment of praise for the spirit of heaviness.
There is a coming day when sorrow ends. Death will be no more. Hold this hope tightly.

lords_prayer.title: The Lord's Prayer Deep Dive
lords_prayer.subtitle: One week unpacking the prayer Jesus taught

schedule.reflection: Not 'My Father' — OUR. You belong to a family. Pray for your church family today.
His name is set apart. Speak it with reverence. Let nothing in your speech today take it lightly.
Pray for the rule of God to break into one specific situation in your life today.

schedule.theme: Give us today our daily bread
schedule.reflection: Daily — not yearly. Trust Him for today's needs without storing in fear.
Confess specifically today. Receive specifically. Forgive specifically.
Where do you most need God's protection this week? Pray it out loud.
End every prayer the way Jesus did — with God's glory, not ours.
